creator: jsegaert@yahoo.com
text:
Update: installing libtorrent-rasterbar (libtorrent-rasterbar-2.0.2 in latest) results into a different error later on, i.e. "Python argument types in torrent_handle.file_progress(torrent_handle) did not match C++ signature"

> root@deluge-1:/var/tmp # cat deluged.log 
> 19:18:25 [ERROR   ][deluge.core.rpcserver         :1413] Exception calling RPC request: Python argument types in
>     torrent_handle.file_progress(torrent_handle)
> did not match C++ signature:
>     file_progress(libtorrent::torrent_handle {lvalue}, libtorrent::flags::bitfield_flag<unsigned char, libtorrent::file_progress_flags_tag, void> flags=0)
> Traceback (most recent call last):
>   File "/usr/local/lib/python3.7/site-packages/deluge/core/rpcserver.py", line 326, in dispatch
>     ret = self.factory.methods[method](*args, **kwargs)
>   File "/usr/local/lib/python3.7/site-packages/deluge/core/core.py", line 768, in get_torrent_status
>     all_keys=not keys,
>   File "/usr/local/lib/python3.7/site-packages/deluge/core/core.py", line 743, in create_torrent_status
>     torrent_keys, diff, update=update, all_keys=all_keys
>   File "/usr/local/lib/python3.7/site-packages/deluge/core/torrent.py", line 1007, in get_status
>     status_dict[key] = self.status_funcs[key]()
>   File "/usr/local/lib/python3.7/site-packages/deluge/core/torrent.py", line 877, in get_file_progress
>     self.handle.file_progress(), self.torrent_info.files()
> Boost.Python.ArgumentError: Python argument types in
>     torrent_handle.file_progress(torrent_handle)
> did not match C++ signature:
>     file_progress(libtorrent::torrent_handle {lvalue}, libtorrent::flags::bitfield_flag<unsigned char, libtorrent::file_progress_flags_tag, void> flags=0)

When reverting back to the quarterly packages (libtorrent-rasterbar-1.2.10 and py37-libtorrent-rasterbar-1.2.10) things work fine.
